items.title: Cross-entropy (information theory)
items.description: Softmax defines model distribution Q over classes; with one-hot true label y, categorical cross-entropy H(P, Q) reduces to −log π_y. See Cross-entropy for the full H(P, Q) definition and link to KL divergence.

items.title: Cross-entropy loss
items.description: Loss functions implement −log π_y on logits via log-softmax internally (e.g. CrossEntropyLoss). Softmax is the activation; cross-entropy is the objective — standard pairing for single-label multi-class training.

items.title: Classification vs regression
items.description: Softmax heads produce discrete class probabilities for classification; regression outputs continuous values without a simplex constraint. Choosing softmax implies mutually exclusive categorical labels.

items.title: Sigmoid activation
items.description: Sigmoid maps one logit to P(y=1) for binary or multi-label problems. Softmax normalizes K logits jointly so probabilities compete and sum to 1 — the right choice when exactly one class is correct.
